text: There may be multiple Kalamus running at once, we need to ensure that the #gui shows this in the browser title as well as the title at the top of the page. Infer the project name either based on package.json or installation directory so the title can be `Kalamu | {PROJECT_NAME}`

Whole-outline PUT (undo/clean) from a stale client strips fields its server's Zod schema doesn't know (default strip mode) - seen 2026-07-10: a pre-assignee tab's Clean up erased assignee fields. Consider passthrough on nodeSchema for PUT, or a version handshake that tells stale UIs to reload

Show on the #gui if the Vite server is not running - some sort of warning so users don't do work and see it disappear into the ether... Shall we disable edits in such a state? Discuss before implementing

Priorities are only usable for tasks, not text notes - when a priority is set (parsing text or command palette) - instead of 'hiding' it for text nodes, in addition to setting the priority, convert the node into a task
Create a #CLI command `kalamu all` that is an alias for `kalamu next --all`
Design + write real landing content (hero, live outline demo island, install, agent story) #landing
Deploy landing to kalamu.dev (CF Pages or Vercel, root dir packages/landing) + DNS at registrar #landing
Kalamu #hub — one always-on local server, all projects in a sidebar (SPEC.md 'Hub' section is canonical)

Project registry ~/.kalamu/projects.json: upsert on every CLI command, slug derivation (package.json name sans scope, else dirname) with numeric-suffix dedupe, stable once assigned, prune missing paths on read, atomic writes, registry failure never breaks the CLI #hub
kalamu hub command: single 127.0.0.1:4400 server, GET /api/projects, lazy per-project createServer() mounts under /p/:slug with idle teardown #hub
Hub sidebar UI in packages/web: project list with open-task counts, switch project by swapping API base path, /p/:slug deep links #hub #gui
kalamu hub install / uninstall: launchd user agent on macOS so the hub starts at login #hub
kalamu open detects a running hub and opens localhost:4400/p/<slug> instead of a standalone server; unchanged fallback #hub
Hub sidebar is render-once: project names/open-task badges only refresh on navigation — consider a hub-global SSE stream for live cross-project badge updates #hub #gui

packages/cli/README.md still documents the legacy self:true flag and @me token (Rules #2, quickstart keys) — update to the assignee model: @human/@agent tokens, assignee field, 'kalamu next skips human-assigned' #docs
A launchd-managed hub keeps running old server code after a CLI update (assets refresh, process doesn't) — consider the hub noticing a newer bundle on disk and exiting so KeepAlive restarts it #hub
